title: Vendor Onboarding Agent
problem: Ops teams manually collect documents, verify details, chase missing information, and email back and forth for weeks before a new vendor is ready to transact.
solution: Agent intakes vendor information, runs parallel document parsing + extraction, sends a structured summary to a human for approval, then updates the vendor master system and notifies all parties.
workflowSteps: Vendor submits onboarding form and uploads documents
Document parser + extraction LLM fire in parallel: extract entity, tax ID, banking details, certifications
Validation: checks for missing required fields, flags potential duplicates in vendor master
Summary sent to human approver with extracted fields and confidence scores
On approval: updates vendor master spreadsheet, emails vendor confirmation, emails admin
On rejection/missing info: emails vendor with specific request for missing data
keyInsights: The typed routing condition is critical: the agent must distinguish between 'missing information' (ask vendor), 'approved' (proceed), and 'disapproved' (reject with reason) — not route based on LLM prose interpretation.

relatedExams.why: Agents and Workflows — conditional routing, HITL
Deploy AI-Powered Business Solutions — procurement automation

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart LR
  A([Vendor Form + Docs]) --> B[Parallel Parse]
  B --> C1[Entity + Tax ID]
  B --> C2[Banking Details]
  B --> C3[Certifications]
  C1 & C2 & C3 --> D[Validate + Dedup Check]
  D --> E[HITL Approval Gate]
  E -->|Approved| F([Update Vendor Master + Notify])
  E -->|Incomplete| G([Request Missing Docs from Vendor])
architectureNotes: Vendor submissions (forms, documents) are ingested and routed through parallel validation agents: sanctions screening, financial health check, document completeness check. A coordinator aggregates results into an overall risk score. Low-risk vendors proceed to automatic approval and ERP provisioning; medium-risk vendors route to procurement review; high-risk vendors are rejected with a documented reason.
